Patient.title: Patient
Patient.description: Represents a patient in the emergency department, including their medical status, diagnosis, and current location. This entity supports real-time monitoring and data management.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Patient entity.

gender.description: The patient's biological sex or gender identity (e.g., Male, Female, Other).

admissionDateTime.description: The timestamp when the patient was admitted to the emergency department.

symptoms.description: A list of initial symptoms reported by or observed in the patient, used for triage and AI suggestions.

triageLevel.description: The assigned triage level for the patient (e.g., Critical, Urgent, Minor, Deceased). Used for dynamic status overviews.

diagnosis.description: The provisional or confirmed medical diagnosis for the patient.

currentStatus.description: The current procedural or administrative status of the patient (e.g., Waiting for X-Ray, In CT Scan, Admitted, Discharged, Deceased). Relevant for resource breakdown widgets.

destination.description: The patient's intended or actual destination after initial ED assessment (e.g., Ward A, ICU, Home, Morgue).

aiTriageSuggestion.description: The AI-powered preliminary triage level suggestion based on reported symptoms, assisting medical staff.

aiDiagnosticPathSuggestion.description: A list of AI-powered suggested diagnostic paths or tests based on patient symptoms.

lastUpdatedDateTime.description: The timestamp when the patient record was last updated, supporting real-time monitoring.

BloodInventory.title: BloodInventory
BloodInventory.description: Manages the stock levels of different blood types available in the facility, displayed in resource widgets.

id.description: Unique identifier for the BloodInventory entity.

bloodType.description: The type of blood (e.g., A+, O-, AB+).

quantityUnits.description: The number of units of this blood type currently available.

lastUpdatedDateTime.description: The timestamp when the blood inventory record was last updated.

Ventilator.title: Ventilator
Ventilator.description: Manages the status and assignment of ventilators within the facility, tracked for usage widgets.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Ventilator entity.

serialNumber.description: The manufacturer's unique serial number for the ventilator device.

status.description: The current operational status of the ventilator (e.g., Available, In Use, Under Maintenance).

patientId.description: Reference to the Patient entity currently using this ventilator. Null if available. (Relationship: Ventilator 1:1 Patient)

assignedDateTime.description: The timestamp when the ventilator was assigned to the current patient.

lastMaintenanceDateTime.description: The timestamp of the last maintenance performed on the ventilator.

definition.description: Stores individual patient records in the emergency department. All documents in this collection are accessible and editable by authenticated medical personnel. Includes critical medical status, diagnosis, and real-time updates.

Manages the stock levels for different blood types available. All documents are accessible and editable by authenticated medical personnel for resource management. The 'bloodTypeId' serves as the unique identifier for each blood type inventory record.

Tracks the status and assignment of individual ventilators. All documents are accessible and editable by authenticated medical personnel for resource monitoring. Each ventilator has a unique 'ventilatorId'.

Database Access Control (DBAC) collection. The existence of a document for a specific userId in this collection signifies that the user holds the 'medical personnel' role, granting them access to operational data. This is crucial for Authorization Independence and efficient QAPs.

firestore.reasoning: The recommended Firestore structure for the Crisis Triage Dashboard prioritizes security, scalability, and debuggability by adhering to the core design principles, especially Authorization Independence and QAPs.The design establishes three primary top-level collections for the application's core entities: `/patients`, `/blood_inventory`, and `/ventilators`. Each collection is designed to hold documents of a single entity type, ensuring structural segregation and a homogeneous security posture.All operational data (patients, blood inventory, ventilators) is intended to be accessible and manageable by 'medical personnel'. Authorization Independence is achieved by making access decisions for any document in these collections reliant solely on the requesting user's global role. This means that security rules will not involve `get()` calls to parent documents or other collections to determine access rights for individual documents within these core collections. Instead, a Database Access Control (DBAC) approach is used: a dedicated collection, `/roles_medical_personnel/{userId}`, will store records indicating which users possess the 'medical personnel' role. The existence of a document for `request.auth.uid` in this collection will grant the necessary read/write permissions.This design directly supports QAPs (Rules are not Filters). Since all documents within `/patients`, `/blood_inventory`, and `/ventilators` are uniformly accessible to `medical_personnel`, any `list` query performed by an authorized user will return all documents matching the query criteria without implicit filtering by security rules. This makes querying predictable and efficient. The structure ensures clarity through semantic naming, explicit state modeling (e.g., `currentStatus`, `triageLevel`), and predictable schemas, facilitating robust security rules that are easy to understand and debug. The Project ID 'er-triage-system' can certainly be used with this proposed Firestore structure.
